title: Evaluating expressions with variables word problems
description: Practice plugging in values to evaluate real-world algebraic expressions. These are introductory problems, so the expressions aren't too complicated.
topic_path: Evaluating expressions with variables word problems

assessment_items.question_content:
An ice cream stand uses the expression $2 + 0.5x$ to determine the cost (in dollars) of an ice cream cone that has $x$ scoops of ice cream.

**How much does an ice cream cone with $5$ scoops cost?** 

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints: The cone has $\blue{5}$ scoops, so $x=\blue{5}$.

Let's substitute $x=\blue5$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}2 + 0.5x\\\\
 &= 2+0.5(\blue{5})\\\\
 &= 2+2.5\\\\
&=\green{4.5}
\end{align*}$
An ice cream cone with $\blue{5}$ scoops costs $\$\green{4.50}$.

options.alt: Ice cream with 5 scoops stacked vertically on a cone.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $100 + 20m$ gives the volume of water in Eduardo’s pool (in liters) after Eduardo spends $m$ minutes filling his pool.

**What is the volume of water in Eduardo’s pool after he fills it for $5 \dfrac14$ minutes?**


[[☃ input-number 1]] liters
hints: Eduardo fills his pool for $\blue{5 \dfrac14}$ minutes, so $m=\blue{5\dfrac14}$.

Let's substitute $m=\blue{5\dfrac14}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}100 + 20m\\\\
 &= 100 + 20\left(\blue{5\dfrac14}\right)\\\\
 &= 100+105\\\\
 &=\green{205}
\end{align*}$
Eduardo's pool will have $\green{205}$ liters of water after he fills it for $\blue{5\dfrac14}$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $12g$ gives the number of kilometers a car can travel using $g$ liters of gasoline.

**How far can this car travel on $5 \dfrac12$ liters of gasoline?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{km}$
hints: A car with $5 \dfrac12$ liters of gasoline tells us that $g=\blue{5\dfrac12}$.
A car can travel $\green{66} \text{ km}$ on $\blue{5 \dfrac12}$ liters of gasoline.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $4x$ gives the perimeter of a square with a side length of $x$ units.

**What is the perimeter of a square with a side length of $\dfrac57$ units?**


[[☃ input-number 1]] units
hints: A side length of $\blue{\dfrac57}$ units tells us that $x=\blue{\dfrac57}$.

Let's substitute $x=\blue{\dfrac57}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}4x\\\\
 &= 4\left(\blue{\dfrac57}\right)\\\\
 &= \green{\dfrac{20}7}\\\\
\end{align*}$
A square with a side length of $\blue{\dfrac57}$ units has a perimeter of $\green{\dfrac{20}7}$ or  $\green{2\dfrac67}$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $62.4d-210$ gives the number of Indian rupees you receive when you exchange $\$d$ at the local currency exchange. 

**How many Indian rupees do you receive when you exchange $\$13$? **


[[☃ input-number 1]]  Indian Rupees
hints: Exchanging $\$13$ tells us that $d=\blue{13}$.

Let's substitute $d=\blue{13}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}62.4d-210\\\\
 &= 62.4\left(\blue{13}\right)-210\\\\
 &= 811.2-210\\\\
 &= \green{601.2}\\\\
\end{align*}$
You receive $\green{601.2}$ Indian rupees when you exchange $\blue{\$13}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The price of a visit to the dentist is calculated according to the formula $50+100n$ where $n$ is the number of cavities the dentist finds.

On your last visit to the dentist, $2$ cavities were found.

 **What was the cost of your visit?**

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Having $\blue{2}$ cavities tells us that $n=\blue{2}$.

Let's substitute $n=\blue{2}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}50+100n\\\\
 &= 50 + 100\left(\blue{2}\right)\\\\
 &= 50+200\\\\
 &=\green{250}
\end{align*}$
The cost of the visit to the dentist will be $\$\green{250}$ if the dentist finds $\blue2$ cavities.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $1.5t + 20$ predicts the height in centimeters of a plant $t$ days from today.

**What is the predicted height of the plant $5$ days from today?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: Predicting the height $\blue{5}$ days from today tells us that $t=\blue{5}$.

Let's substitute $t=\blue{5}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}1.5 + 20\\\\
 &= 1.5(\blue5) + 20\\\\
 &= 7.5 + 20\\\\
 &=\green{27.5}
\end{align*}$
The predicted height of the plant $\blue5$ days from today is $\green{27.5} \text{ cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $1.08s + 1.02b$ predicts the end-of-year value of a financial portfolio where $s$ is the value of stocks and $b$ is the value of bonds in the portfolio at the beginning of the year.

**What is the predicted end-of-year value of a portfolio that begins the year with $\$200$ in stocks and $\$100$ in bonds?**

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Beginning the year with $\$\blue{200}$ in stocks and $\$\pink{100}$ in bonds tells us that $s=\blue{200}$ and $b=\pink{100}$.

Let's substitute $s=\blue{200}$ and $b=\pink{100}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}1.08s + 1.02b\\\\
 &= 1.08(\blue{200})+1.02(\pink{100})\\\\
 &= 216+102\\\\
&=\green{318}
\end{align*}$
A financial portfolio that begins the year with $\$\blue{200}$ in stocks and $\$\pink{100}$ in bonds is predicted to be valued at $\$\green{318}$ at the end of the year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $20l + 25g - 10$ gives the number of dollars that Josie makes from mowing $l$ lawns and raking $g$ gardens.

**How many dollars does Josie make from raking $4$ gardens and mowing $3$ lawns?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] dollars
hints: Josie raking $\blue{4}$ gardens and mowing $\pink{3}$ lawns tells us that $g=\blue{4}$ and $l=\pink{3}$.

Let's substitute $g=\blue{4}$ and $l=\pink{3}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}20l + 25g -10\\\\
 &= 20(\pink{3})+25(\blue{4}) - 10\\\\
 &= 60 + 100 - 10\\\\
 &= \green{150}
\end{align*}$
Josie makes $\green{150}$ dollars from raking $\blue4$ gardens and mowing $\pink3$ lawns.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $\dfrac12bh$ gives the area of a triangle where $b$ is the base of the triangle and $h$ is the height of the triangle.

**What is the area of a triangle with a base of $7 \text{ cm}$ and a height of $4 \text{ cm}$?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints: A base of $\blue7 \text{ cm}$ and a height of $\pink4 \text{ cm}$ tells us that $b=\blue7$ and $h=\pink4$.

Let's substitute $b=\blue{7}$ and $h=\pink{4}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}\dfrac12bh\\\\
 &= \dfrac12(\blue{7})(\pink{4})\\\\
 &= \dfrac12(28)\\\\
 &= \green{14}
\end{align*}$
A triangle with a base of $\blue7 \text{ cm}$ and a height of $\pink4 \text{ cm}$ has an area of $\green{14} \text{ cm}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular prism with length $\ell$, width $w$, and height $h$ has a volume of $\ell w h$.

**What is the volume of a prism which has a base of $5\text{ m}$ by $3\text{ m}$, and a height of $4\text{ m}$?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ m}^3$
hints:
A prism which has a base of $\blue5\text{ m}$ by $\pink3\text{ m}$ and a height of $\purple4\text{ m}$ tells us that $\ell=\blue{5}$, 
$w=\pink{3}$, and $h=\purple{4}$.

Let's substitute $\ell=\blue{5}$, 
$w=\pink{3}$, and $h=\purple{4}$ into the expression 
and evaluate:  


$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}\ell wh\\\\
 &= \blue{5}(\pink{3})(\purple4)\\\\
 &= \blue5(12)\\\\
 &= \green{60}
\end{align*}$
The prism with a base of $\blue5\text{ m}$ by $\pink3\text{ m}$ and a height of $\purple4\text{ m}$ has a volume of $\green{60}\text{ m}^3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In basketball, the number of points you score is given by $3x + 2y + 1z$ where $x$ is three-pointers, $y$ is two-pointers, and $z$ is free throws (one-pointers).

**What is the total number of points scored by a player who makes $3$ three-pointers, $5$ two-pointers, and $6$ free throws?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] points
hints:
A player making $\blue3$ three-pointers,  $\pink5$ two-pointers,  and $\purple6$ free throws tells us that $x=\blue{3}$, 
$y=\pink{5}$, and $z=\purple{6}$.

Let's substitute $x=\blue{3}$, 
$y=\pink{5}$, and $z=\purple{6}$ into the expression 
and evaluate:  


$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}3x + 2y + 1z\\\\
 &= 3(\blue{3})+2(\pink{5})+1(\purple6)\\\\
 &= 9+10+6\\\\
 &= \green{25}
\end{align*}$
A player making $\blue3$ three-pointers,  $\pink5$ two-pointers,  and $\purple6$ free throws would score a total of $\green{25}$ points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ben, Cam, and Justin are lumberjacks. The number of trees they chop down is given by $b+2c+3j$ where $b$ is the number of hours Ben spends chopping, $c$ is the number of hours Cam spends chopping, and $j$ is the number of hours Justin spends chopping.

**How many trees do they chop down after Ben spends $8$ hours chopping, Cam spends $3$ hours chopping, and Justin spends $4$ hours chopping?**


[[☃ input-number 1]]  trees
hints: Ben chopping for $\blue8$ hours,  Cam chopping for $\pink3$ hours,  and Justin chopping for $\purple4$ hours tells us that $b=\blue{8}$, $c=\pink{3}$, and $j=\purple{4}$.

Let's substitute $b=\blue{8}$, 
$c=\pink{3}$, and $j=\purple{4}$ into the expression 
and evaluate:  


$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}b+2c+3j\\\\
 &= \blue{8}+2(\pink{3})+3(\purple4)\\\\
 &= 8+6+12\\\\
 &= \green{26}
\end{align*}$
They chop down a total of $\green{26}$ trees after Ben spends $\blue8$ hours chopping, Cam spends $\pink3$ hours chopping, and Justin spends $\purple4$ hours chopping.

assessment_items.question_content:
Calvin and Melvin are two of Santa's elves.  The number of toys they build is given by $11c+8m$ where $c$ is the number of candy canes Calvin eats for breakfast and $m$ is the number of candy canes Melvin eats for breakfast.

**How many toys do they build when Calvin eats $5$ candy canes and Melvin eats $3$ candy canes for breakfast?**

[[☃ input-number 1]]  toys
hints: Calvin eating $\blue5$ candy canes and  Melvin eating $\pink3$ candy canes tells us that $c=\blue5$ and $m=\pink3$.

Let's substitute $c=\blue{5}$ and $m=\pink{3}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}11c+8m\\\\
 &= 11(\blue{5})+8(\pink{3})\\\\
 &= 55+24\\\\
 &= \green{79}
\end{align*}$
They build $\green{79}$ toys when Calvin eats $\blue5$ candy canes and  Melvin eats $\pink3$ candy canes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bethany sells roses and petunias. The expression $3r+2.5p$ gives the cost (in dollars) of $r$ roses and $p$ petunias.

**What is the cost of $7$ roses and $8$ petunias?**

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Purchasing $\blue7$ roses and $\pink8$ petunias tells us that $r=\blue7$ and $p=\pink8$.

Let's substitute $r=\blue{7}$ and $p=\pink{8}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}3r+2.5p\\\\
 &= 3(\blue{7})+2.5(\pink{8})\\\\
 &= 21+20\\\\
 &= \green{41}
\end{align*}$
It costs $\$\green{41}$ to purchase $\blue7$ roses and  $\pink8$ petunias.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eric owns and operates the Hot Ham food truck.  The expression $3.25b+2h$ gives the cost of $b$ burgers and $h$ hot dogs.

**What is the cost of $4$  burgers and $6$ hot dogs?**

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Purchasing $\blue4$ burgers and $\pink6$ hot dogs tells us that $b=\blue4$ and $h=\pink6$.

Let's substitute $b=\blue{4}$ and $h=\pink{6}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}3.25b+2h\\\\
 &= 3.25(\blue{4})+2(\pink{6})\\\\
 &= 13+12\\\\
 &= \green{25}
\end{align*}$
It costs $\$\green{25}$ to purchase $\blue4$ burgers and $\pink6$ hot dogs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lois and Clark own a company that sells wagons.  The amount they pay each of their sales employees (in dollars) is given by the expression $12h+30w$ where $h$ is the number of hours worked and $w$ is the number of wagons sold.


**What is the amount paid to an employee that works $6$ hours and sells $3$ wagons?**

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Working $\blue6$ hours and selling $\pink3$ wagons tells us that $h=\blue6$ and $w=\pink3$.

Let's substitute $h=\blue{6}$ and $w=\pink{3}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}12h+30w\\\\
 &= 12(\blue{6})+30(\pink{3})\\\\
 &= 72+90\\\\
 &= \green{162}
\end{align*}$
A sales employee gets paid $\$\green{162}$ for working $\blue6$ hours and selling $\pink3$ wagons.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ben Frank is an electrician who installs lighting fixtures. He charges (in dollars) his clients $15h+40f$ where $h$ is the number of hours worked and $f$ is the number of fixtures installed.

**How much does Ben Frank charge when he works $7$ hours and installs $9$ fixtures?**

$\$$[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints: Working $\blue7$ hours and installing $\pink9$ fixtures tells us that $h=\blue7$ and $f=\pink9$.

Let's substitute $h=\blue{7}$ and $f=\pink{9}$ into the expression and evaluate:

$\begin{align*}
&\phantom{=}15h+40f\\\\
 &= 15(\blue{7})+40(\pink{9})\\\\
 &= 105+360\\\\
 &= \green{465}
\end{align*}$
Ben Frank charges $\$\green{465}$ when he works $\blue7$ hours and installs $\pink9$ fixtures.
